$comment: Shared MCP surface contract for the local plane (walkerOS packages/mcps/mcp) and the hosted plane (app src/lib/mcp). Both planes pin their own copy of this file and assert the tools they actually expose against it, so a rename, an added or dropped field, a changed description, or a reworded hint fails a test on the side that drifted instead of silently diverging. Edit it only together with the matching copy in the other repo.

observeSession.name: observe_session
observeSession.title: Observe Session
observeSession.description: Open, inspect, or end an Observe session: a time-boxed window on one flow that runtimes attach to as arms. A preview arm streams from a browser, a container arm runs server-side, and both feed ONE shared journeys feed. start opens the window (arms picks which runtimes attach), status reports per-arm state plus recordsReceived and expiresAt, stop ends the whole session including every arm. A flow has at most one session, so status/stop resolve it from flowId when sessionId is omitted. Read the events with observe_journeys; this tool never returns event data and never judges whether events are correct.

action.description: start opens a session, status reports arm state, stop ends the whole session.

flowId.description: Flow the Observe session runs on.

projectId.description: Project ID. Optional; falls back to the default project.

sessionId.description: Session to act on for status/stop. Optional; the flow has at most one session and it is resolved for you.

arms.description: Which runtimes attach. Omit to attach the default preview arm; a web settings that references a server flow brings its container arm with it.

container.description: Pass true to attach the server container arm, which selects the flow's server settings when no preview arm is named. Only true is accepted: a web settings that references a server flow always brings its container arm along, so a container cannot be suppressed here.

preview.description: Name the flow settings this session observes. A web settings attaches the browser preview arm (plus the container arm of any server flow it references); a server settings attaches the container arm alone. Omit to use the flow's single web settings.

origins.description: Bare https origins (https://host[:port]) the session may ingest web events from.

level.description: Container observation verbosity. Defaults to the app's own.

replace.description: Replace the flow's existing window instead of attaching to it. Re-provisions from the new config.

hints.SIMULATE_FIRST: Simulate before preview: flow_simulate checks mapping with no browser.
hints.PREVIEW_STREAMS: Preview streams into this session: mint a link with flow_manage preview_regrant, then open it on your site.
hints.READ: Read with observe_journeys (flowId); it is the only read.
hints.STOP: End both arms with observe_session stop.
hints.EMPTY_FEED: recordsReceived is 0: nothing has reached the feed yet. Drive traffic on an attached arm.
hints.ENDED: Session ended and both arms detached. Start a new one with observe_session start.
hints.NO_WINDOW: No Observe session on this flow. Open one with observe_session start.
